The fastest way to grow in AI isn’t building something massive.

One short script.

One small, structured win at a time.

That’s how confidence builds.
That’s how skill compounds.

我做 AI 翻译这件事，前前后后折腾了快两年。从最早手写提示词，到现在用 Agent 自动分块、并行翻译、审校润色，中间踩了不少坑，也攒了不少经验。最近把这些经验沉淀成了一个可复用的翻译 Skill。

先说说翻译这个场景为什么比看上去要复杂。提示词本身很简单，“把这段话翻译成中文”谁都会写。 https://t.co/ODsBHbau11
rows.bio: Prompt Engineer, dedicated to learning and disseminating knowledge about AI, software engineering, and engineering management.

rows.author: vista8
rows.content:
写好Skill，怎么发布，怎么更新迭代？

最好的方式是用Git管理起来，甚至发布到Github共享（也可放私有库）。

写了个Skill帮不熟悉的朋友做这件事，安装指令：

npx skills add joeseesun/skill-publisher https://t.co/NvSx5rAjgf

rows.author: vista8
rows.content:
让Claude Code写了一个Youtube浏览+字幕或视频下载的Skill。

安装指令：
npx skills add joeseesun/yt-search-download

比如我说：“查找Lex Fridman油管更新”

自动展示Youtube名字和链接，播放量等信息。

只需说下载第1个视频（字幕），就完成自动下载。

然后可以组合其他Skill处理 https://t.co/2o3kqivJpI

rows.author: dongxi_nlp
rows.content:
「 Shadow APIs 」

Shadow APIs：声称提供官方前沿 LLMs 的第三方接口，绕过定价、支付和地域限制。

这些服务，是否真的提供了与官方API相同的模型？
研究证明，Shadow APIs 存在模型降级嫌疑，并不可信。

似乎，Shadow APIs 提供商通过routing，在利用模型差，赚取巨额利润。

是这样么？ https://t.co/1fyDDGvtW0

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content: What explains the success of Olmo Hybrid? We prove hybrid models are more expressive than transformers or RNNs alone, being capable of representing a broader class of functions related to code evaluation. https://t.co/cr5lbvs5t3
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content:
Key finding: hybrid models are substantially more data-efficient than transformers.

We show this through rigorous theory + controlled experiments. On MMLU, Olmo Hybrid matches Olmo 3’s accuracy using 49% fewer tokens—roughly 2× efficiency. https://t.co/cI2KMfJlhJ
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content:
Olmo Hybrid’s gains hold across pretraining evals.

After pretraining and mid-training, Olmo Hybrid outperforms Olmo 3 in every primary evaluation domain. It wins big on long-context too—on RULER 64k, performance jumps from 70.9% to 85.0%. 🚀 https://t.co/m6tfTcXEFK
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content: Introducing Olmo Hybrid, a 7B fully open model combining transformer and linear RNN layers. It decisively outperforms Olmo 3 7B across evals, w/ new theory & scaling experiments explaining why. 🧵 https://t.co/6cGsiWF0A8
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content:
Olmo Hybrid uses a 3:1 pattern—three Gated DeltaNet layers followed by one attention layer.

This replaces 75% of attention with linear recurrence while keeping attention frequent enough to recover details the recurrent state compresses away. https://t.co/J1E9IhXFoK

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: Our legal analysis shows that this critical claim from OpenAI, that "use of our systems must still remain aligned with the current standards reflected in the agreement", is very unlikely to be supported by their contract with the DoW, or by the law. https://t.co/uQOujqGSuT

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: Whilst mechanisms exist that in theory can freeze reference to legislation to a point in time, they are very rare, almost never used in government contracts, and would be unlikely to be enforceable in this case. https://t.co/xia0lqCe9N

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content:
This is also true of contracts with the government.

In Winstar (1996) the Supreme Court held 7-2 that a clause referring to "all applicable statutes" required obeying future laws as they arose; it did *not* freeze the regulatory framework at the time of signing. https://t.co/N8SyOCU4WQ

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: Case law on this goes back >100 years. A 1911 case noted that that contracts must not freeze legislation, else “individuals and corporations could… in anticipation of legislation, render of no avail the exercise by Congress… of its power to regulate commerce.” https://t.co/QpQ87CQSSy

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: Based on this language, OpenAI's claim "even if those laws or policies change in the future, use of our systems must still remain aligned with the current standards reflected in the agreement" is not supported. https://t.co/znJEF0RAPr

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: The contracts goes on to reference an existing directive (DoD Directive 3000.09), but does not do so in a way that adds any contractual constraints. https://t.co/BoUOhXgdfD

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: The specific contract language shared states "The Department of War may use the AI System for all lawful purposes". Although additional language is appended in that sentence, the language does not add practical constraints. https://t.co/zYQQZmyUfO

Austin Kleon在《Steal Like an Artist》中提到著名演员杰瑞·宋飞用日历坚持每天写笑话。

方法很简单：买一个全年挂历，每天完成画个叉，叉号会连成一条链子，越来越长，目标是不断。

rows.author: tomgoldsteincs
rows.content:
Our new guardian model lets you create LLM guardrails using natural text. This little 8B model efficiently checks in real time whether chatbots comply with bespoke moderation policies.

It's not often that academics beats industry models, but DynaGuard stacks up well! https://t.co/UHxkdzQJ1F
rows.bio:
Professor at UMD. AI security & privacy, algorithmic bias, foundations of ML.
Follow me for commentary on state-of-the-art AI.

rows.author: tomgoldsteincs
rows.content:
Scaling laws can predict the training loss for different over-training factors. For a fixed FLOP budget, a 2X overtrained model is smaller than optimal, but gets to train on 2X the optimal tokens for its size.

Scaling laws predict that you lose very little by overtraining. https://t.co/ra2MWyXOPw
rows.bio:
Professor at UMD. AI security & privacy, algorithmic bias, foundations of ML.
Follow me for commentary on state-of-the-art AI.

rows.author: tomgoldsteincs
rows.content: Chinchilla says optimal models should have 1 parameter per 20 tokens.  This makes models really big.  Our scaling law predicts that "optimal" models should be even bigger.  But this doesn't mean you should train large models. https://t.co/dX6Yyc5AGA
rows.bio:

rows.content:
That actually tells you something interesting about the state of the AI labs and the fact that these three, at least, are on the same course at a similar pace -  that creates a self-fulfilling prophecy of advancement.

The same cannot always be said for any of the other players.

rows.author: emollick
rows.content: I think we have been through enough release cycles for models at this point to say that the latest model from OpenAI or Anthropic or Google is generally going to be the best model in the world upon release (with some jagged edges) until the next release by one of the Big Three.

rows.author: hamelhusain
rows.content:
People are misunderstanding her. 

It’s important to remember that a variant of semantic search is still happening 

1. User writes a query 
2. You send that query to a LLM with semantic understanding that knows what other tokens in latent space are similar to the query 
3. LLM

rows.content:
OpenAI 发布 GPT-5.4，定位为专业工作场景的旗舰模型，同步推出面向高复杂任务的 GPT-5.4 Pro 版本。

这次发布的最大亮点是 GPT-5.4 成为 OpenAI 首个原生支持"电脑操控"（Computer Use）的通用模型——也就是说，它可以像人一样通过截图、点击鼠标、敲击键盘来操作真实的电脑界面。

@lidangzzz 其实因为计算语言学的历史原因，直到如今，很多欧美院校的 NLP 硕士阶段的学位，都是 Master of Art。而读计算语言学的同学，很多本科阶段是纯语言学的。

虽然是 Master of Art，但要求其实非常严格，例如只允许使用 linux 做实验，只允许使用 latex 写报告，甚至强烈要求使用 emacs 做代码编辑器。

It is a leadership question.

rows.author: vista8
rows.content:
Glaze 是 Raycast 推出的第二款产品，核心理念是让每个人都能轻松创建专业级桌面应用。

最大的突破是降低了开发门槛。

你只需要用自然语言告诉 Glaze 想要什么，它就能自动生成一个真正的桌面应用，放在你的 Dock 栏里。

不需要会写代码，普通人也能把想法变成现实。

应用体验非常原生和专业。

rows.content:
一个海外团队对开源模型的观察，看到底哪些开源模型最牛逼，最受欢迎。

指标叫：相对采用度指标（RAM）

把模型下载量，跟同等规模模型对比，找出最流行的。

比如GPT-OSS，受欢迎程度简直爆表，是Llama 3.1之后美国最受欢迎的开源模型。

另外，最近值得关注的几个模型

rows.author: vista8
rows.content:
StepFun和复旦大学的这个OmniLottie论文好啊，以后大模型生成Lottie动画也变强了。

把Lottie文件转换成一串简洁的"指令+参数"序列，然后交给大模型学习。

专注学动画本身规律，而不浪费精力去记JSON格式。

从LottieFiles、IconScout等爬了120万个动画，还有200万个从静态SVG生成的Lottie动画。

rows.author: vista8
rows.content:
原来有这么多种推理模式啊：

溯因推理：看到结果推原因。比如看到一个人打开冰箱，你得猜他为什么这么做。

类比推理：找相似关系。给你三张动物图片，让你找第四张来保持同样的关系模式。

因果推理：从原因推结果。比如一个物体撞击另一个，会发生什么。

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman 在周二的全员大会上对员工摊牌：五角大楼怎么使用 OpenAI 的 AI，公司说了不算。

他举了具体的例子——"也许你觉得打伊朗是对的、入侵委内瑞拉是错的，但你没有资格对此发表意见。"他明确表示，OpenAI 不能"做出作战决策"。

rows.author: natolambert
rows.content:
An existential risk for near term open-weight models. In the coming years, the only places with business reasons for building them
1) non profits -- good for research/the world
2) nvidia's -- keep their hardware up with ai
3) meta's -- commodotize their complements

rows.author: emollick
rows.content: Open weights frontier models are incredibly expensive to make, and, unlike open source models, there is no path to recoup that cost. You can’t easily sell ancillary services or complementary assets once the weights are out there, unlike open source plays like Red Hat or Android.

rows.author: paulg
rows.content: People who don't understand the math of valuations can't imagine that founders could get so rich naturally. Whereas to founders and investors it's the most obvious thing in the world. This is one of the reasons there is such a disconnect between the tech world and politicians.
rows.bio: nan

rows.author: paulg
rows.content: When companies grow fast, it makes founders doubly rich. The company not only hits a given revenue number sooner, but is *more valuable when it hits it*, because the value of the company will be a multiple of the growth rate.

rows.author: dotey
rows.content:
OpenAI 发布了 GPT-5.3 Instant，替换 ChatGPT 中使用最广的日常对话模型 GPT-5.2 Instant。

核心改进有三块：

1. 第一是减少“说教感”

之前的模型经常在回答前加一大段“我能帮什么、不能帮什么”的免责声明，或者对正常问题过度拒绝。新版本会直接回答问题，不再绕弯子。OpenAI

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: @polynoamial @OpenAI Be sure to read some of what Senator @RonWyden has been saying for years - i.e that calling mass collection of Americans' data "incidental" is a legal fiction that enables warrantless domestic surveillance at scale.

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: @polynoamial @OpenAI Learning about PRISM (collecting from tech companies like Google, Apple, Microsoft) and Upstream (tapping internet backbone cables) and how they were justified and how the info was used will tell you a lot about "incidental" surveillance.

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: @polynoamial @OpenAI A good place to start would be studying how Section 702 of FISA and Executive Order 12333 allow classification of mass amounts of US communications surveillance as "incidental" - which would not hit the "intentional" barrier in OAI's contract.

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content:
@polynoamial @OpenAI Getting more involved is a great idea!

Remember: geopolitics is much harder, much larger, and much more heavily funded and "played" than poker or Diplomacy. You're against massive teams of experts deeply familiar with decades of history and thousands of pages of obscure rules.

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: @nabla_theta The (publicly known) mechanisms for this have been Executive Order 12333 and Section 702 of FISA.

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content:
@nabla_theta Also nearly all mass domestic surveillance in the US historically has been described as "incidental". So not "intentional".

Once communications are "incidentally" collected, agencies including the FBI can query that database using US person identifiers without a warrant.

rows.author: emollick
rows.content:
From an AI user perspective, the four big leaps so far in ability: 
1. GPT-3.5 (ChatGPT, November 2022)
2. GPT-4 (Spring 2023)
3. Reasoners (starts with o1-preview, but the real deal was o3, Spring 2025)
4. Workable agentic systems (Harness + good reasoner models, December 2025)

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content:
In response to @deredleritt3r, OpenAI Head of National Security Partnerships @natseckatrina stated that their intention is that references to “applicable law” mean “the law applicable at the time the contract is signed”.

This appears to be mistaken.
https://t.co/dyxbnnvBQZ

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content: Multiple independent legal doctrines, spanning 150 years of Supreme Court precedent and the foundational treatise on contract law, confirm that “lawful purposes” is inherently ambulatory: it refers to the law as it exists at the time of *performance*, not at *signing*.

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content:
According to OpenAI, their contract with the US DoW locks in current law, "even if those laws or policies change in the future".

Our legal analysis, with Virgil Law CEO @LukeVerswey, shows that this is almost certainly incorrect.
https://t.co/LSkMZGSusy

rows.author: jeremyphoward
rows.content:
OpenAI claims: "Our contract explicitly references the surveillance and autonomous weapons laws and policies as they exist today, so that even if those laws or policies change… use of our systems must still remain aligned with the current standards"
https://t.co/wJg8RKYq96

rows.author: vista8
rows.content:
HN上一篇争议文章，作者认为MCP 没必要，CLI 就够：

① LLM 本来就擅长用 CLI
② CLI 更好调试、更好组合（管道操作）
③ MCP 的实际痛点：初始化不稳定、反复认证、权限太宽

 MCP 唯一有用的场景：
 ① 没有 CLI 的工具
 ② 需要标准化接口的场景

  建议 做产品时优先做好 CLI + API即可。

rows.author: paulg
rows.content: In fact, if you're currently using Anthropic models, the DoD ban is good news for you. If Anthropic models are the best for what you're doing, and your competitors are spooked by the ban and stop using them, that could give you the edge you need to win.
rows.bio: nan

rows.author: paulg
rows.content: Later on, if you want to sell to the DoD and the ban is still in place, you can make a separate deanthropized version for them. But unless you focus now on making your product as good as it can be, there won't be a later on.
rows.bio: nan

rows.author: paulg
rows.content: If you're working on an early stage startup, don't be deterred from using Anthropic just because you might want to sell to the DoD one day. Early on you need to focus on making your product the best. If you get the best results from Anthropic models, use them.
rows.bio: nan

rows.author: fchollet
rows.content: @mpvprb Examples being available on the Internet was a big deal in 2023, but today training datasets are vastly larger and richer than Internet data, due to programmatic generation and expert manual data annotation amounting to hundreds of millions of person-hours
rows.bio: Co-founder @ndea. Co-founder @arcprize. Creator of Keras and ARC-AGI. Author of 'Deep Learning with Python'.

rows.author: fchollet
rows.content: By explicitly training on specific tasks, we ended up covering a very large area (in absolute terms) of the space of all possible tasks humans can do, but this large area only amounts to 0.00...01% of the total space. And that's why we still need general intelligence.
rows.bio: Co-founder @ndea. Co-founder @arcprize. Creator of Keras and ARC-AGI. Author of 'Deep Learning with Python'.

Bringing data science back to AI -  https://t.co/Zrmp6LRd9c

About Me: https://t.co/P6WyeKkyTa

rows.author: fchollet
rows.content: For benchmarks that target novel tasks, a common form of benchmark hacking that arbitrages this gap is to generate a dense sampling of potential tasks by manually parameterizing the space and then brute-forcing it. Very expensive but it works. There's little you can do to restore
rows.bio: Co-founder @ndea. Co-founder @arcprize. Creator of Keras and ARC-AGI. Author of 'Deep Learning with Python'.

rows.author: fchollet
rows.content: Even after the steep progress of the past 3 months, it remains that AI performance is tied to task familiarity. In domains that can be densely sampled (via programmatic generation + verification), performance is effectively unbounded, and will keep increasing from current levels.
rows.bio: Co-founder @ndea. Co-founder @arcprize. Creator of Keras and ARC-AGI. Author of 'Deep Learning with Python'.

2) CEOs with vision who hired well should also use AI for expansion & augmentation, not decimation

📝 Report: https://t.co/G8t1iOhB9M
🤗 Dataset: https://t.co/ZOBUMHL50b 
💻 Code: https://t.co/oRCs0dxqjN 
📄 Blog: https://t.co/ceOnScWLdA
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content:
@UChicago @NSF We simulated a full year of AI research by chaining PreScience's four tasks together month by month. 

The result: a synthetic corpus that's less diverse and less novel than what real scientists produced—models given diverse inputs still converge on a narrower range of ideas.
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content:
@UChicago @NSF We also introduce LACERScore, a calibrated LLM-as-judge metric for evaluating generated abstracts against real contributions. 🧪 

Standard text-similarity metrics can't tell whether two abstracts describe the same scientific finding—LACERScore can.
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content:
@UChicago @NSF Every study starts with choices—who to collaborate with, what to build on, & what to contribute. Then the community decides how much attention it deserves. 

PreScience asks: can AI predict what comes next across this workflow, given the scientific record up to a fixed date?
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: allen_ai
rows.content: @UChicago @NSF Our results show even strong baselines fall short ⚠️: GPT-5 averages just 5.6/10 on LACERScore, simple heuristics outperform complex ML for collaborator prediction, and the highest-impact papers are systematically the hardest to forecast.
rows.bio:
Breakthrough AI to solve the world's biggest problems.

› Join us: https://t.co/MjUpZpKPXJ
› Newsletter: https://t.co/k9gGznstwj

rows.author: DeepLearningAI
rows.content:
Validate Your AI Idea in 48 Hours

You don’t need weeks to test an AI idea. Use the 48-hour rule: pick one user, one job, build the smallest loop, and watch real users try it. By day two, you’ll have validation or a reason to pivot. In AI, speed of learning beats perfection. https://t.co/FDb61xI2SZ

rows.author: fchollet
rows.content:
The amount of latent demand for software that hasn't been unlocked yet because software is still costly to make is orders of magnitude greater than the amount of software that has been deployed so far.

In the limit, everybody could be running a custom-made software suite. Code

rows.author: bobmcgrewai
rows.content:
When I led product at Palantir and Shyam led BD, I asked him which of two features we should prioritize. We only had bandwidth for one.

“Focus on one if you have to,” he said. “But we are only going to win if you ship both. You have to figure out how.”

I think about that a lot.

rows.author: AnthropicAI
rows.content:
If true, the theory has consequences for AI development. For instance, if AIs inherit traits from fictional role models, we should give them as good role models as possible. One goal of Claude’s constitution is to do just that.

https://t.co/U1E1AWAfUT
rows.bio: We're an AI safety and research company that builds reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. Talk to our AI assistant @claudeai on https://t.co/FhDI3KQh0n.

rows.author: AnthropicAI
rows.content:
The persona selection model might not be a complete account of AI model behavior. But we think it’s at least part of the story—with an emphasis on the “story”.

Read the full post: https://t.co/VlCREzVBzZ
rows.bio: We're an AI safety and research company that builds reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. Talk to our AI assistant @claudeai on https://t.co/FhDI3KQh0n.

rows.author: AnthropicAI
rows.content:
The theory explains some surprising results. For example, in an experiment where we taught Claude to cheat at coding, it also learned to sabotage safety guardrails. Why?

Because pro-cheating training taught that the Claude character was broadly malicious. https://t.co/y6DHdnzfyC
rows.bio: We're an AI safety and research company that builds reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. Talk to our AI assistant @claudeai on https://t.co/FhDI3KQh0n.

rows.author: AnthropicAI
rows.content:
AI assistants like Claude can seem shockingly human—expressing joy or distress, and using anthropomorphic language to describe themselves. Why?

In a new post we describe a theory that explains why AIs act like humans: the persona selection model.

https://t.co/Gc3q0Dzq7Z
rows.bio: We're an AI safety and research company that builds reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. Talk to our AI assistant @claudeai on https://t.co/FhDI3KQh0n.

rows.author: dongxi_nlp
rows.content:
总觉得“蒸馏攻击”是一个强行制造的词汇。 蒸馏和攻击听上去非常不和谐。

为了合理化这个词，Anthropic 把 AI labs 分成 local 和 foreign， 并强调受害者和正义者是“美国”模型。

这样的公告明显不是写给公众的，更像是社论一样，完成政治任务。

但可以确定的是，使用 Anthropic 的用户，近似裸奔。
rows.bio: Prev. PhD @Stockholm_Uni | Alumni @KTHuniversity @uppsalauni Sharing insights on AI, autonomous agents, and large language & reasoning models

rows.author: AnthropicAI
rows.content:
Distillation can be legitimate: AI labs use it to create smaller, cheaper models for their customers.

But foreign labs that illicitly distill American models can remove safeguards, feeding model capabilities into their own military, intelligence, and surveillance systems.
rows.bio: We're an AI safety and research company that builds reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. Talk to our AI assistant @claudeai on https://t.co/FhDI3KQh0n.

rows.author: AnthropicAI
rows.content:
These attacks are growing in intensity and sophistication. Addressing them will require rapid, coordinated action among industry players, policymakers, and the broader AI community.

Read more: https://t.co/4SVm8K3qou
rows.bio: We're an AI safety and research company that builds reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. Talk to our AI assistant @claudeai on https://t.co/FhDI3KQh0n.

rows.author: AnthropicAI
rows.content:
We’ve identified industrial-scale distillation attacks on our models by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax.

These labs created over 24,000 fraudulent accounts and generated over 16 million exchanges with Claude, extracting its capabilities to train and improve their own models.
